title: Tyngsborough man may face charges after allegedly taking police boat, posing as officer
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 63-year-old Tyngsborough man allegedly took a police boat onto Lake Mascuppic, activating its emergency lights.
Authorities say the man attempted to enforce local boating rules, using the police boat without authorization.
Tyngsborough police plan to summon the man for impersonating an officer; a court will decide on formal charges.

title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels participated in the Parade of Sail into Boston Harbor.
The USS Constitution led ships from more than 20 countries, concluding their Sail250 tour in Boston.
Thousands of visitors gathered at various waterfront locations to witness the maritime event.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox select shortstop Jake Schaffner from UNC with No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th prospect by MLB.com, hit .356/.467/.552 with six homers and 50 RBI in 68 games.
Defensively strong, Schaffner committed three errors with a .987 fielding percentage and stole 26 bases in 2026.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: Sail Boston 2026 featured the Grand Parade of Sail with historic ships led by the USS Constitution.
The event marks a generational tribute to maritime history, showcasing ships like the clipper Flying Cloud.
Notable vessels included the schooner Adventure from Gloucester and Canada's replica schooner Bluenose Two.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello named midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts before being demoted to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite poor starter stats, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA and was not recalled to replace an injured starter.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Boston Red Sox, currently on a seven-game winning streak, are close to an American League wild-card spot.
Boston faces the decision to buy at the trade deadline for a postseason push or sell assets for future gains.
Past indecision at trade deadlines in 2024 and 2025 led to underwhelming results for the Red Sox.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira's TD Garden performance, part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour, included songs from her Grammy-winning album.
The concert highlighted Shakira's personal journey through her divorce and legal battles, celebrating strength and resilience.
Setlist included hit tracks like 'Hips Don't Lie' and 'Waka Waka', along with an emotional performance of 'Acróstico.'

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Healey-Driscoll administration awards $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects include converting historic buildings in Fall River and Lawrence into rental apartments.
Since 2023, HDIP has awarded tax credits for 52 projects, creating over 3,400 homes in 16 cities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, arrested in Albany, NY, linked to armed bank robberies in NH and NJ last month.
Sawyer allegedly stole $6,000 from St. Mary’s Bank and $12,000 from a Chase Bank using a semiautomatic handgun.
The arrest was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving FBI and multiple state law enforcement agencies.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, found dead near his motorcycle in Shelburne, NH, after missing since July 4.
A resident searching for Grondin discovered his body along Route 2 on Thursday night.
Preliminary investigation shows Grondin crossed lanes and struck a post; no other vehicle was involved.
